---
title: "Survival & Duration Analysis"
subtitle: "Time-to-event modeling with censoring"
---

## Overview

How long until a discharged patient is readmitted? Until a treated condition relapses? Until an unemployed worker finds a job? These time-to-event questions have a feature that breaks ordinary regression: at the end of follow-up, many subjects simply *have not had the event yet*. We do not know their event time --- only that it exceeds the time observed. This is censoring, and discarding or ignoring those subjects biases everything.

Survival analysis is built to handle exactly this. The module starts with the language of survival and hazard functions and the nonparametric Kaplan-Meier curve that respects censoring, then develops the Cox proportional-hazards model --- the semiparametric workhorse that gives interpretable hazard ratios without committing to a baseline shape --- and finishes with parametric duration models (where the hazard's shape itself is the object of interest) and competing risks (where more than one kind of event can end the spell).

## Pages in This Module

- [Survival Analysis: Hazards and the Kaplan-Meier Curve](survival-basics.qmd) --- Modeling time-to-event with censoring.
- [The Cox Proportional Hazards Model](cox-proportional-hazards.qmd) --- Semiparametric regression for hazards.
- [Parametric Duration and Competing Risks](parametric-competing-risks.qmd) --- Shapes of hazards and multiple event types.
